title: Endovier
summary: A brutal salt-mine prison camp used by Adarlan for forced labor.

evidence.note: Celaena’s imprisonment and survival in Endovier are established.

title: The King’s Champion
summary: A covert operative selected through a royal competition to carry out missions for the King of Adarlan.

evidence.note: Dorian explains the competition, service term, and promise of freedom.

title: The Glass Castle
summary: The royal seat in Rifthold, built partly from glass and rising above the city.

evidence.note: Celaena sees and recalls the castle while approaching Rifthold.

title: Lady Lillian Gordaina
summary: The alias assigned to Celaena during the Champion competition to conceal that Dorian’s contestant is Adarlan’s Assassin.

evidence.note: She is introduced to the other competitors under the false name.

title: The Walking Dead
summary: An ancient book associated with Wyrdmarks and knowledge that ordinary court histories do not explain.

evidence.note: Celaena encounters the book and its unfamiliar marks.

title: Wyrdmarks
summary: Ancient symbols tied to a deeper form of power. At this point in the story, their complete rules and origins remain deliberately unclear.

evidence.note: The marks become identifiable as part of the mystery surrounding The Walking Dead.

title: Wyrdgates
summary: Celaena’s research links Wyrd to gates between the living world and other realms, though she does not yet understand how those gates operate.

evidence.note: Her library research introduces competing ideas about Wyrd and Wyrdgates.

title: Elena and Gavin’s tomb
summary: A hidden tomb beneath the castle preserves the memory of Queen Elena and King Gavin alongside maps of the earth, stars, and ancient Wyrdmarks.

evidence.note: Celaena finds the tomb through the passage behind her rooms.

title: The Eye of Elena
summary: A protective amulet associated with Elena and Gavin. Elena gives it to Celaena while warning her about the evil inside the castle.

evidence.note: Celaena awakens from the tomb encounter holding the amulet.

title: Deanna’s golden arrow
summary: During Yulemas rites, Celaena receives the token of Deanna, goddess of the hunt and protection of the young.

evidence.note: A child in the ceremonial procession presents Celaena with the arrow.

title: The ridderak
summary: A predatory creature summoned through Wyrdmarks and fed with human sacrifices. Its victims’ strength can be transferred to the person controlling it.

evidence.note: Celaena identifies the ritual and discovers Cain performing it.

title: Damaris
summary: The legendary sword of Gavin Havilliard, preserved in the hidden tomb and used by Celaena against the ridderak.

evidence.note: Celaena takes Gavin’s sword from the tomb during the ridderak attack.

title: Elentiya
summary: An Eyllwe name Nehemia gives Celaena after learning her identity. It means Spirit That Could Not Be Broken.

evidence.note: Nehemia gives Celaena the name as the friends reconcile.

title: Bloodbane
summary: A poison that weakens Celaena during the final duel while also making the supernatural forces around the clock tower visible to her.

evidence.note: Celaena recognizes the poisoning as her perception expands beyond the ordinary world.

title: The clock tower Wyrdgate
summary: The black clock tower is more than architecture: its marks form a gate through which Cain’s shadow creatures can reach the duel.

evidence.note: The bloodbane allows Celaena to recognize the tower’s supernatural function.

title: The black rings
summary: Matching black rings are associated with the king, Perrington, and Cain. Perrington uses their power to alter Cain’s body and manipulate Kaltain’s mind.

evidence.note: The king and Perrington discuss the rings’ influence over Cain and Kaltain.

title: Wyrd practice in Eyllwe
summary: Nehemia reveals that members of Eyllwe’s royal family still practice Wyrd magic in secret despite Adarlan’s suppression of magic.

evidence.note: Nehemia explains her healing, protective marks, and efforts against Cain’s summons.

title: Elena’s blood tie
summary: Elena tells Celaena that their bond is one of blood. The exact family history and its consequences remain unrevealed at this point.

evidence.note: Elena explicitly describes their connection as a blood tie.

title: Mort and surviving ancient magic
summary: Mort’s awareness proves that old enchantments can continue functioning even after ordinary magic vanished from Adarlan.

evidence.note: Mort speaks and explains that ancient magic and spells can endure.

title: Dorian’s raw magic
summary: Dorian possesses untrained power even though magic has been absent from Adarlan for a decade. Its survival makes him both unusual and vulnerable.

evidence.note: Dorian can no longer dismiss the uncontrolled manifestations around him.

title: The three Wyrdkeys
summary: Three fragments of the Wyrd were made into keys capable of opening gates between worlds and granting enormous power when reunited.

evidence.note: Baba Yellowlegs explains their origin, purpose, and danger.

title: The Valg
summary: The Valg are beings from another realm who once sought the Wyrdkeys. At this point, their full history and connection to current events remain unknown.

evidence.note: Baba Yellowlegs names the Valg while recounting the Wyrdkeys’ history.

title: Ironteeth witch marks
summary: The scars left by Baba Yellowlegs identify Celaena as the killer of a powerful Ironteeth witch and could draw retaliation from her kind.

evidence.note: Mort identifies the meaning of the scars.

title: How magic was suppressed
summary: Evidence beneath the castle indicates that the king used a Wyrdkey in the larger act that drove ordinary magic from his conquered lands.

evidence.note: The empty hiding place and Mort’s explanation connect the lost key to the king.

title: Celaena’s Fae form
summary: Beyond the portal, Celaena takes on Fae features and wields fire, revealing that the ancestry she concealed has a living magical expression.

evidence.note: Chaol witnesses the transformation and blue fire.

title: Celaena is Aelin Galathynius
summary: Chaol’s research confirms that Celaena Sardothien is Aelin Galathynius, the surviving heir of Terrasen.

evidence.note: The date, ancestry, transformation, and royal records lead Chaol to the truth.
